name: Wallis and Futuna (France)

colors.symbolism: the field of the local flag
the white square enclosing the canton and the saltire of Wallis on the fly
the blue stripe of the French Tricolore in the canton, indicating French sovereignty

description: A red field with a white square in the upper hoist containing the French Tricolore as a sub-canton, and a white saltire-like cross on the fly side.
history: The official flag of Wallis and Futuna is the French Tricolore; this red local flag with the saltire of Wallis is the unofficial territorial flag in widespread use. Wallis and Futuna became a French protectorate in 1888 and an Overseas Territory (TOM) in 1961, and is now a French Overseas Collectivity (COM).

features.symbolism: the red field of the historic territorial flag of Wallis and Futuna
French sovereignty over the territory; Wallis and Futuna is a French overseas collectivity
the four armed saltire associated with the Kingdom of Wallis (Uvea), one of the three traditional kingdoms of the territory
